claims.claim: Content density is defined as the ratio of information visible compared to the space available in the interface, and two discrete modes are supported: 'comfortable' (default) and 'compact' (for data-intensive views).
claims.quote: Content density is defined by the ratio of information visible compared to the space available in the interface. Comfortable is the standard density level of the system, active by default. Compact is an additional density level for data intensive views.

claims.claim: Comfortable mode must always be set as the default because compact mode can hinder readability, overwhelm users, and prolong content consumption.
claims.quote: Always set comfortable mode as default. Comfortable mode is the default density level of Cloudscape. Compact mode can hinder readability, overwhelm, and prolong content consumption.

claims.claim: In compact mode, the vertical spacing scale is reduced in increments of 4px (using a 4px base unit), decreasing spacing both inside components and between components.
claims.quote: In compact mode, the spacing scale is reduced in increments of 4, decreasing the vertical spacing inside components and between components.

claims.claim: Compact density is deliberately not applied to informational components (help panels, alerts, form validation) in order to maintain readability in those areas.
claims.quote: Not fully applied to informational components like help panels, alerts, and form validation messages to maintain readability.

claims.claim: Comfortable mode is positioned as superior for cross-device experiences, not just desktop use, implying density defaults must account for multi-device contexts.
claims.quote: Comfortable mode optimizes content consumption and readability, as well as cross device experiences.
